areaReviews: Queenstown is Singapore's first satellite town, developed in the 1950s by the SIT. It offers mature HDB estates alongside newer condominiums, popular with families and young professionals.
Excellent MRT connectivity via the East-West Line (Queenstown EW19, Commonwealth EW20) and Circle Line access at Buona Vista (CC22). Buses connect to Orchard Road in ~15 minutes.
The area features abundant green spaces: Kent Ridge Park, HortPark, and the Southern Ridges trail network. Queenstown Stadium and Swimming Complex provide sports facilities.
Shopping options include IKEA Alexandra, Anchorpoint, Queensway Shopping Centre, and the vibrant Holland Village nearby. Multiple hawker centres serve affordable local food.
Close to NUS, Singapore Polytechnic, and one-north/Biopolis tech cluster, making it a top choice for students, researchers, and tech professionals.
